rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE RANKIN VS. HANNA EDGAR NEVINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Deft'S 998 Offer, To Vacate Entry Of Judgment And To Stay Execution Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT HANNA NEVINS' Motion To Set Aside Defendant's 998 Offer, To Vacate Entry Of Judgment And To Stay Execution Of Judgment. Denied. Defense counsel's asserted failure to recognize plaintiff in intervention New Hampshire Insurance Company ("NHIC ") as a party to the action and not a lienholder, and defense counsel's omission of the language "inclusive of any and all liens" on the 998 offer were not clerical mistakes and not the type of mistakes ordinarily made by a person with no special training or skill. Defendant has not met its burden of establishing grounds for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). See Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 259-60; Pazderka v. Caballeros Dimas Alang, Inc. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 658, 671 - 672. Plaintiff's counsel's failure to include NHIC on the proof of service of plaintiff's response to the 998 offer is not a basis to grant the CCP 473(b) motion, especially where as here the defendant's proof of service of the 998 offer itself showed service only on plaintiff Rankin. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAK HING YU VS. HIN DUN KONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT KE ZHU, EAST RAINBOW CONSTRUCTION INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. Cross-Complainant has leave to amend to attach exhibit C to the cross-complaint. Zhu and East Rainbow's demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege a basis for liability, e.g., conspirator, agent, aider and abettor. The reference to B&P Code § 7114 is misplaced because that is a disciplinary statute. The Court overrules East Rainbow's demurrer to the second cause of action for violation of B&P Code § 7031. Cross-Complainant sufficiently alleges that East Rainbow was unlicensed and received Cross-complainant's money. The Court sustains Zhu's demurrer to the second cause of action without leave to amend. The cross-complaint alleges that Zhu was licensed and there is no allegation that he received cross-complainant's money. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL L. HUYGHUE VS. WILLIAM R. HAMBRECHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Renewed Mtn For Charging Order And Assigment Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S Renewed Motion For Charging Order And Assignment Order. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to continue in part and grant in part. As to the motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to WR Hambrecht/Private Equity Investments, LLC, WR Hambrecht Ventures II Management, LLC, Hambrecht Suma Capital I Management, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Aristotle 2 Management, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Aristotle Management, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Odyssey, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Twitter, and Granite Ventures, LLC, the Court is inclined to continue the hearing to give Defendant an opportunity to respond to the new evidence Plaintiff presented in reply. As to the motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to H&Q Ariat Investment Mgmt. Co., LLC, and W.R. Hambrecht/VIZU, LLC, the Court is inclined to continue to allow Plaintiff to present more facts about the chronology of the liquidations. The motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to all other limited liability companies, the three limited partnerships, both bank accounts, and the real property rental is unopposed and therefore granted. The parties are requested to bring to the hearing courtesy copies of the unredacted versions of their moving and opposing papers and supporting declarations to the extent these papers have been conditionally sealed. Consistent with the Court's tentative views, the parties are to meet and confer and bring to the hearing a proposed form of order granting an assignment and charging order for the uncontested entities/accounts/rental properties. The parties are to meet and confer regarding a date for the continued hearing on the remainder of the motion. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JANE GELFAND VS. POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examiniation Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER's Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff. Granted for good cause shown. In her complaint, Plaintiff alleges that she suffers and will continue to suffer pain, mental anguish, and emotional distress as a direct and proximate result of Defendant's actions. Plaintiff's deposition testimony establishes that she continues to suffer from mental, emotional and psychological distress. Further, Plaintiff continues to be treated professionally for anxiety and depressive symptoms that she asserts are related to the substance of her claims. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: ALLISON JOHNSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY*****
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF SOCIAL SERVICES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second - fourth causes of action only. (CCP § 430.10(e).) State of California is immune from liability under the licensing immunity per Gov. Code § 818.4 for the alleged violations of Health & Safety Code §§ 1522.1, 1534, 1538. Not every instance of the term "shall" implicates a mandatory duty that is enforceable by a private plaintiff. (MacDonald v. St. of Calif. (1991) 230 Cal.App.3d 319, 330.) Unlike Corona v. State of California, the Department here exercises its discretion in determining whether to issue, revoke or deny a license under Health & Safety Code §§ 1522.1, 1534, 1538. (Corona v. State (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 723, 728 - 729, 732.) To the extent that Plaintiff requests leave to amend to add additional causes of action against the municipal defendants, the request made in opposition to State of California's demurrer is insufficient. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST VS. TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANTS SHINE PROPERTIES, LLC, WWJW FAMILY, LLC, TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. The Court takes judicial notice of the settlement agreement. The settlement agreement is reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation and the allegations of the amended complaint do not conflict with the agreement. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"); see also Martinez v. Socoma Co., Inc., (1974) 11 Cal.3d 394, 400. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRIA PORTILLO VS. SONOMA TIRES, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 26, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTS SONOMA TIRES, INC., MISSION TIRES, INC., JACK RHIEL IV'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Denied. Venue is proper in San Francisco County because Defendant Mission Tires, Inc. is a resident of San Francisco County. Code of Civil Procedure § 395. Defendants have not yet answered and cannot make a motion to transfer pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 397( c) based on the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice. Even if it were timely, moving Defendants have not met their burden. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 396(b), Plaintiff is entitled to an award of attorney fees and costs in the amount of $2500. Counsel for Defendants Sonoma Tires, Inc., Mission Tires, Inc. and Jack Rhiel IV shall pay this amount. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
